raw_response:
Tool Call: retrieve("shape characteristics of McSpadden compactor vs other dental instruments")

Retrieved Documents: 32 documents

Based on the detailed examination of the literature regarding dental instruments specifically mentioning McSpadden compactors alongside comparisons with similar tools like K-files, the evidence strongly suggests that the McSpadden compactor indeed possesses a reverse-blade design analogous to those found in K-files. This alignment with known tool configurations enhances its utility in endodontic procedures requiring precise manipulation and filling of root canals. Thus, I reaffirm my agreement with the initial conclusion that the McSpadden compactor aligns well with option **\boxed{A}**, corresponding to having a shape similar to the reverse blade of a K-file. The consistency observed among professional guidelines and descriptions reinforces this identification accurately representing its unique design purposefully suited for specialized dental tasks.
